description: This lecture discusses instance optimality in algorithms, focusing on the 2D maxima problem, where the goal is to identify undominated points from a set of input points in the plane. The lecture highlights an algorithm from the mid-80s that achieves strong instance optimality guarantees.
topic_summary: The lecture covers instance optimality and the 2D maxima problem, explaining how to identify undominated points among given input points. It emphasizes the significance of comparison-based algorithms and the goal of achieving an instance optimal algorithm.
